Authorized by Republican Central


Committee of Spokane County

JUDGE TURNER INSULTED


LABOR|NC MEN

pages.raw_text:
Senator George Turner, now a cam
didate for the suffrages of the whole
people, now the alleged friend of or
ganized labor, and now the foe of
aristocracy. capitals and corpora
tions, has forgotten. perhaps, the
part he played in a certain demon
stration known to old residents as
'Bread Riot Day" when he told the
representatives OF organized labor
on the floor of public meeting, that
their presence was an insult to the
meeting. and asked that he be named
on committee Of five sergeants-at-
arms to see that they removed their
dirty presence" from his view.


Mr.. Turner was offended because
tax-paying laborers desired certain
public moneys taken from the banks,
where they benefited only n few
financiers. and expended for the good
Of the iabOring people. the munich
pality and the community at large.
He had attempted by injunction to
prevent this action, and when the
great body of citizens rose and de-
manded action he attempted, unavail-
sngiy. to stem the tide Of events.
